race.name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France
race.tagline: 110km of Chilean coastal fury with 1,200m punch—Tour de France soul hits the Southern Hemisphere!

vitals.registration: Opens early 2026 via official site; expect €150-250 including finisher's jersey, support, and Tour prestige
vitals.prize_purse: No cash; glory, medals, and Tour de France swag for top finishers
vitals.aid_stations: 6-8 fully stocked with gels, bars, hydration, and Chilean empanadas every 15-20km
vitals.cutoff_time: 8-10 hours for masses; broom wagon mercy

climate.description: November brings mild 15-22°C days with high humidity from Valdivia's rivers and coast. Expect occasional drizzle turning roads slick but refreshing the soul. Sunny breaks highlight Andean foothills in emerald glory.

terrain.primary: Coastal rollers through lush Valdivian rainforest
terrain.surface: Smooth asphalt with occasional chipseal; riverside paths wind through ancient alerce forests. Fresh pavement expected for debut, but watch for wet leaves and gravel shoulders. Pure Southern Cone poetry on two wheels.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Debut event carries Tour de France pedigree into exotic Chile, but unproven logistics cap it at T3. Adventure and prestige shine bright for amateurs seeking new frontiers. Needs a few editions to build legend status.

logistics.explanation: Valdivia airport is small but direct from Santiago; expect shuttle chaos for debut. Lodging in this gem town books fast. Tour orgs will nail it, but Southern Chile remoteness demands planning.

length.explanation: 110km hits sweet spot—not France's masochistic 170km monsters, but enough to hurt. Perfect for sub-pros building base. Short option saves face for mortals.

technicality.explanation: Paved paradise, low descents, minimal corners. Wet coast roads add spice, but no Alpe d'Huez hairpins. Beginners thrive; experts yawn at tech.

elevation.explanation: 1,200m is appetizer, not feast. Rollers nibble, no soul-crushers. TdF branding oversells the vert—fine for fun, weak for climbers.

climate.explanation: Mild temps, but humidity and rain pummel morale. Coastal gales test resolve. Pack arm warmers; embrace the Patagonian edge.

altitude.explanation: Sea level snooze. No hypoxia drama. Pure power test, zero acclimation needed—blessing or curse for high-altitude addicts.

adventure.explanation: Chile's wild south: rainforests, rivers, coast. TdF in the Americas? Bucket-list gold. Far-flung vibe cranks exotic factor to 11.

prestige.explanation: Tour de France Series stamp elevates instantly. Jersey screams legitimacy. Debut hype draws global eyes—wear it proud.

race_quality.explanation: ASO polish guaranteed: closed roads, pro timing, epic feed zones. Chile twist adds flavor. First-year kinks possible, but pedigree strong.

experience.explanation: TdF immersion minus peloton pressure. Finisher photos at iconic arches. Valdivia party post-ride seals the memory.

community.explanation: Chilean cycling scene passionate but nascent. International draw builds buzz. Local hospitality warms the gaps.

field_depth.explanation: Amateur masses, no elites yet. South American speedsters mix with tourists. Grows with editions.

value.explanation: Prestige per euro unbeatable. Inclusions stack up. Travel splurge aside, pure steal.

expenses.explanation: Chile flights pricey from Europe/US. Local costs low. Budget fly-and-ride works.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Chile blasts Tour spirit into Chile's lush south, blending coastal beauty with manageable suffering. Debut 110km from Niebla immerses you in rainforest rollers under Tour branding—no French Alps brutality, but fresh adventure. Skeptics yawn at low vert; purists drool over novelty. Valdivia's charm post-ride rivals any podium.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Signature entry for Southern Hemisphere TdF dreams. Nail training, dodge rain—claim your jersey. World needs more events like this.

final_verdict.one_liner: TdF magic meets Chilean wilderness—worth the trek!
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if craving exotic road prestige without Alpine agony. Skip if chasing mega-vert or elite competition.
final_verdict.alternatives: Unreal Gran Fondo (unreal-granfondo), L'Etape Costa Rica (letape-costa-rica), TransAndes Challenge (transandes)

logistics.airport: Fly into Valdivia (ZOS) or connect via Santiago (SCL, 1hr flight). Shuttles from Niebla start; taxis cheap.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book Valdivia hotels/B&Bs early—riverside gems abound. Event camps likely. Avoid peak summer surge.
logistics.food: Empanadas, fresh seafood, craft beer explosion. Vegan options growing. Post-race asados legendary.
logistics.packet_pickup: Niebla or Valdivia expo day prior.
logistics.parking: Ample at coastal start; carpool encouraged.

history.founder: ASO (Tour de France organizers) expanding L'Etape Series
history.origin_story: Born from TdF's global conquest, L'Etape Chile plants the yellow jersey in Patagonia. First Southern Cone edition targets nature-loving amateurs. Valdivia chosen for epic coast-river fusion. Debut promises closed roads and pro vibes.
history.reputation: Tour pedigree ensures quality. Chile's debut builds hype. Early buzz solid from series fans.

course_description.character: Coastal launch from Niebla rockets into Valdivia's emerald valleys. Rollers twist through ancient forests, rivers flashing beside. Gentle climbs build to panoramic payoffs—no endless grinds, all scenic reward. TdF signage elevates every pedal.

suffering_zones.label: Valdivia River Punch
suffering_zones.desc: First real test: 5km grind paralleling the Calle-Calle. Legs awaken amid rainforest humidity. Crowds cheer from bridges.

suffering_zones.label: Coastal Headwind Hell
suffering_zones.desc: Open bluffs batter with Pacific gusts. Grind solo or echelon up. Mental breaker mid-race.

course_description.signature_challenge: The Niebla-to-Valdivia coastal traverse: 110km essence in rainforest-wrapped rollers. 1,200m spread thin but relentless, with humidity amplifying burn. TdF finish line in Valdivia seals the conquest.

guide_variables.race_name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France

non_negotiables.why: 110km demands steady power over rollers. Humidity spikes effort 20%. Test rainy 4hr rides to mimic. Nail this or suffer broom wagon.

non_negotiables.requirement: Pack rain kit and arm warmers
non_negotiables.why: November deluges inevitable. Slick roads crash-prone. TdF pride demands finish—gear saves the day.
